summary: Comprehensive Playwright UI testing of the Greenlight Studio marketing site (Next.js 16 App Router, static site, no backend). All 15 review-request items were validated on both desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844) viewports. The page renders cleanly with no JS errors, all data-testids present, all anchors point to correct hrefs, all section content matches the spec, scroll-triggered Reveal animations resolve to opacity:1 once in view, mobile layout stacks without horizontal scroll. Only one minor design discrepancy with the spec found: the navbar GitHub icon is hidden on mobile (CSS class `hidden sm:inline-flex`) whereas the spec expected it visible at 390px alongside logo + CTA.

ui_bugs.issue: navbar-github icon is hidden on mobile viewports (<640px) due to `hidden sm:inline-flex` Tailwind class. Spec says mobile should still show 'logo + CTA + GitHub'. Either remove `hidden` so it shows from base, or update the spec. Low priority — GitHub link is still reachable via the footer.

issues: 4 Next.js font preload warnings: 'resource was preloaded using link preload but not used within a few seconds from the window's load event'. These are common Next.js font optimization warnings and do not affect functionality, but the team may want to investigate `next/font` preload tuning.

verified_features: Page loads at root URL with all 8 sections present (navbar, hero, product-preview, features, philosophy/why, roadmap, pricing, download, footer). No console errors. HTTP 200.
Hero headline = 'Production Management.\nReinvented.' with 'Reinvented.' rendered as italic green (font-display italic gl-green-text class confirmed).
Hero subhead, small text, primary CTA ('Download Android Beta' -> #download), secondary CTA ('View Features' -> #features) all present and correct.
Smooth scroll: clicking hero-primary-cta brings download-section into viewport; clicking hero-secondary-cta brings features-section into viewport.
All 5 navbar nav-link-* selectors exist with correct hrefs (#features, #why, #roadmap, #pricing, #download).
navbar-cta 'Get the Beta' -> #download. navbar-github -> https://github.com/KingCarni/Greenlight-Webpage with target=_blank.
screenshot-0 / 1 / 2 each present with an inside loading from /screenshots/*.jpg.
All 6 feature cards present with correct kebab-case testids (asset-library, room-scanner, project-dashboard, ai-assistant, cloud-sync, team-collaboration).
All 8 roadmap items present in exact order: Android Beta (Shipped), Asset Library, Room Scanner, Project Dashboard, AI Automation (In progress), iOS App, Web Portal, Marketplace (Planned).
Pricing headline 'Pricing will be announced closer to launch.' rendered; pricing-cta 'Request Early Access' -> #download confirmed.
download-android shows version v0.1.0-beta; download-apk-button href = https://expo.dev/artifacts/eas/fq0q6eIotkCO3GuwP9JXMhvlnf7rtPB64EZyA-BvYjQ.apk with target=_blank.
download-ios and download-desktop cards both render 'COMING SOON' (CSS uppercase styling of 'Coming Soon') with Notify Me <button disabled>.
site-footer contains 'Greenlight Studio', '© 2026 Greenlight Studio', and footer-link-github -> https://github.com/KingCarni/Greenlight-Webpage target=_blank.
Mobile (390x844): nav-link-* are all hidden (md:flex), navbar-logo & navbar-cta visible, no horizontal scrollbar (documentElement.scrollWidth == clientWidth).
Scroll-triggered Reveal: pricing-cta resolves to computed opacity=1 after IntersectionObserver fires (~2s buffer). roadmap-item-0 opacity=1 after scrolling to roadmap section.
action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Decide whether navbar GitHub icon should be visible on mobile. Currently `hidden sm:inline-flex` hides it below 640px. If the spec is the source of truth, remove `hidden` (replace with `inline-flex`). Otherwise update the spec — not blocking.
OPTIONAL/COSMETIC: Look into Next.js font preload warnings (4 .woff2 files preloaded but flagged as 'not used within a few seconds'). These are warnings only and do not break anything.
critical_code_review_comments: lib/content.ts is a clean single source of truth for all copy — good pattern.
Reveal.tsx uses IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-60px' and threshold 0.05 — solid approach, no Framer Motion dependency, lightweight.
Hero, Download, Pricing, Roadmap, FeatureGrid, ProductPreview, Footer all are well-structured client components with proper data-testid coverage on every interactive element.
Navbar GitHub icon uses `hidden sm:inline-flex` which contradicts the spec saying GitHub should be visible alongside logo + CTA on mobile. Minor inconsistency.
External links (APK, GitHub) correctly use target=_blank + rel='noopener noreferrer'. Good.
Disabled iOS/Desktop 'Notify Me' buttons use proper `disabled` attribute + opacity-60 + cursor-not-allowed. Good.
No console errors at runtime — only Next.js font preload warnings (cosmetic).
No backend / no /api endpoints — pure static marketing site, as described.

success_rate.frontend: 98% (1 minor mobile GitHub-icon visibility discrepancy out of 15 requirements)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: None — static site, no data layer.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This is a static Next.js marketing site at the /app root (NOT /app/frontend). No backend, no /api routes. Preview URL: https://ba74791f-b638-4880-ad62-9e097816b78f.preview.emergentagent.com. All interactive elements have kebab-case data-testid attributes. Reveal animations use IntersectionObserver — give ~1.5–2s buffer after scrolling before asserting opacity. iOS/Desktop 'Notify Me' buttons are intentionally disabled. The COMING SOON labels render uppercase via CSS — use case-insensitive matching or compare against 'COMING SOON'. Mobile breakpoint hides both nav-links (md:flex) and navbar-github (sm:inline-flex), so on 390px viewport only logo + CTA are visible.
rca of the issue: Minor mobile navbar GitHub visibility mismatch: Navbar.tsx line 90 uses `hidden sm:inline-flex` on the GitHub anchor. Tailwind's `sm` breakpoint is 640px, so at 390px the icon is display:none. Fix (if needed): change `hidden sm:inline-flex` to `inline-flex` so it stays visible at all viewports.
